news.headline: Phibro Animal Health Corp (Nasdaq: PAHC) to attend Bank of America 2025 Virtual Animal Health Summit on Feb 27th at 10:50 AM ET.
news.summary: Phibro Animal Health Corporation (Nasdaq: PAHC) has disclosed its upcoming participation in the Bank of America 2025 Virtual Animal Health Summit. The company's Chief Financial Officer, Glenn David, is scheduled to speak to financial analysts and investors on Thursday, February 27, 2025, at 10:50 AM ET. The live audio presentation can be accessed through the Phibro Animal Health Corporation Investor Relations Website at https://investors.pahc.com. Following the event, a replay of the session will also be made available.

news.headline: Brown-Forman (NYSE: BFA, BFB) declares quarterly cash dividend of $0.2265 per share, payable on April 1, 2025.
news.summary: In Louisville, Kentucky, Brown-Forman Corporation (NYSE: BFA, BFB) has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.2265 per share on its Class A and Class B Common Stock. This dividend will be payable on April 1, 2025, to shareholders of record on March 7, 2025. Being a part of the S&P 500 Dividend Aristocrats index, Brown-Forman has a strong dividend history, having paid regular quarterly cash dividends for 81 years and consistently increasing the cash dividend for 41 consecutive years.

news.headline: Sprouts Farmers Market reports strong results for Q4 2024 and fiscal year ending Dec 29, 2024. CEO Jack Sinclair praises company's success and growth.
news.summary: Sprouts Farmers Market, Inc., based in Phoenix, Arizona, recently released their financial results for the 13-week fourth quarter and 52-week year ending on December 29, 2024. The company, listed as Nasdaq: SFM, saw significant success in 2024 according to Jack Sinclair, the CEO. Sinclair praised the company's performance and stated that their strategic efforts positioned them well for future achievements. He highlighted that their distinct, attribute-focused product line has gained popularity among their intended customer base.

news.headline: Marshall Rose, key figure in rejuvenating NYC institutions, including NYPL and Bryant Park, passes at 88.
news.summary: His efforts played a key role in rejuvenating the New York Public Library and converting Bryant Park from a once unsafe area into a beautiful oasis.

news.headline: Insulet's 2024 revenue up 22% for full year and 17% for Q4. "Incredible year with strong growth," says President.
news.summary: Insulet Corporation, a leader in tubeless insulin pump technology with its Omnipod® brand, reported its financial performance for Q4 and FY ending December 31, 2024. Jim Hollingshead, President and Chief of Insulet, expressed satisfaction with the strong fourth quarter and overall year, surpassing growth and margin targets. The company, listed on NASDAQ as PODD, marked notable achievements in the period.

news.headline: Veradigm extends its Stockholder Rights Plan with a new expiration date of August 2025.
news.summary: Veradigm® (MDRX), a top provider of healthcare data and tech solutions, recently announced that its Board of Directors has approved an extension of the Company’s existing Stockholder Rights Plan. Initially adopted on February 26, 2024, the Rights Plan was set to expire on February 26, 2025. However, with the approved amendment, the expiration date has been extended to August 20. This move indicates the company's continued dedication to shareholder protections and governance.

news.headline: ITF Therapeutics presents 7 abstracts at MDA conference, showing givinostat's effects, safety, disease progression reduction.
news.summary: Seven abstracts were presented showing the consistent effectiveness, long-term safety, and decrease in disease progression with givinostat. The results also indicated a delay in respiratory decline with the medication. The data was shared in CONCORD, Mass on Feb. 20, 2025 by ITF Therapeutics LLC, the U.S. affiliate.

news.headline: Palladyne AI Corp shares 2024 financial update, with $40.1 million in cash and marketable securities.
news.summary: Palladyne AI Corp. (NASDAQ: PDYN and PDYNW) disclosed significant business and financial milestones for 2024 alongside the submission of its 2024 Annual Report on Form 10-K. The AI developer for robotic platforms in industrial and defense sectors reported enhanced financials, boasting $40.1 million in cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities. This improved balance sheet reflects a positive year for the company.

news.headline: Veradigm (OTCMKTS: MDRX) expands board with Kent Lake PR LLC, aligning with shareholders' input on board composition by Jan 30, 2025.
news.summary: In Chicago, Veradigm, a healthcare data and technology solutions provider (OTCMKTS: MDRX), has recently signed a Cooperation Agreement with Kent Lake PR LLC. This agreement, in response to shareholder input on board composition on January 30, 2025, aims for an orderly refreshment of the Board post exploration of strategies. The Board seeks to enhance its structure with insights from this agreement.

news.headline: Akamai secures $100M+ deal as cloud provider for tech giant, enhancing cyber security and cloud infrastructure.
news.summary: In Cambridge, Massachusetts on February 20, 2025, Akamai Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ: AKAM), a leading cybersecurity and cloud computing firm, revealed the completion of a significant multi-year strategic usage deal with a major global technology company. The agreement is aimed at enhancing online security and cloud services for businesses.

news.headline: Weave (NYSE: WEAV) reports strong Q4 and full year 2024 financial results, with improvements in gross margin and operating income.
news.summary: In Lehi, Utah, Weave (NYSE: WEAV) disclosed its financial performance for Q4 and FY ending December 31, 2024. The company, known for its comprehensive customer experience and payments software for small to medium-sized healthcare businesses, reported positive outcomes. There were enhancements in gross margin, cash flow, and operating income (loss), showcasing robust market demand and operational efficiencies. The company's CEO expressed satisfaction with the results, emphasizing a successful quarter and year.

news.headline: Montrose Environmental Group appoints Vincent Colman to Board of Directors and Audit Committee as of Feb. 19, 2025.
news.summary: On February 20, 2025, in Little Rock, Arkansas, Montrose Environmental Group, Inc. (NYSE: MEG), a leading worldwide environmental solutions provider, disclosed the selection of Mr. Vincent Colman to join its Board of Directors and Audit Committee, as of February 19, 2025. This decision marks a significant development for the company as it continues to strengthen its governance structure.

news.headline: Weave (NYSE: WEAV) CFO Alan Taylor to retire at end of Q1, with Jason Christiansen as successor. #leadershiptransition
news.summary: In Lehi, Utah, a prominent customer experience and payments software platform for small and medium-sized healthcare businesses, Weave (NYSE: WEAV), shared news that its Chief Financial Officer Alan Taylor is set to retire at the close of Q1 in 2025. Jason Christiansen, the company's Vice President of Finance, is in line to succeed Taylor and step into the CFO position, becoming part of the executive team post his retirement. This transition marks a planned shift after Alan Taylor's tenure of four years.

news.headline: Japan’s innovative toilet jokes are thriving, pushing boundaries in humor.
TPI Composites, Inc. announces improved profitability in 2025 after reporting Q4 and full year results for 2024.
news.summary: On February 20, 2025, in Scottsdale, Arizona, TPI Composites, Inc. (Nasdaq: TPIC) announced their financial performance for the fourth quarter and full year ending December 31, 2024. The company disclosed key figures regarding their financial results for that period without specifying the details.

news.headline: Texas Roadhouse increases quarterly dividend to $0.68 per share by 11% and approves $500 million stock repurchase program.
news.summary: The company has decided to raise its quarterly dividend by 11%, resulting in a new rate of $0.68 per share. Additionally, they have approved a stock repurchase program totaling $500 million. This move shows a commitment to returning value to shareholders and confidence in the company's financial position. The increase in dividend and the stock repurchase program highlight the company's positive outlook and focus on enhancing shareholder returns.

news.headline: Micron Technology set to report fiscal Q2 results on March 20, 2025 at 2:30 p.m. Mountain time. Webcast available.
news.summary: Micron Technology, Inc., based in Boise, Idaho and listed on NASDAQ as MU, is set to host its fiscal Q2 earnings conference call on Mar. 20, 2025, at 2:30 p.m. MT. Those interested can join the live webcast at http://investors.micron.com/. For up to a year post-call, webcast replays will be available on Micron's Investor Relations site. Micron is known for being a top player in cutting-edge memory technology. For more details, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Dynavax had a successful 2024, with HEPLISAV-B revenue growing 26% ($268M); 2025 guidance includes revenue of $305-$325M. Top-line Phase 1/2 shingles trial results expected by Q3 2025. Share repurchase program to complete in 2025. Plague vaccine Phase 2 trial to initiate in Q3. Top-line 2024 revenue was $71.1M with a 39% increase YoY, while Full Year 2024 revenue was $277.2M. Adjusted EBITDA also increased significantly in 2024. Conference call on Feb 20, 2025. Business updates also noted.
news.summary: Dynavax Technologies Corporation reported a net product revenue of $268 million for HEPLISAV-B in 2024, a 26% increase from the previous year, with expected revenue of $305-325 million in 2025. Top-line results for a shingles trial are anticipated in Q3 2025, and a Phase 2 trial for a plague vaccine will start in the same quarter. The company expects to complete a $200 million share repurchase program in 2025. In the fourth quarter of 2024, total revenues were $72 million, a 30% increase year-over-year, with an estimated $713.8 million in cash and marketable securities. Full-year 2024 revenues were $277.2 million, a 19% increase from 2023. Adjusted EBITDA for 2024 was $51.9 million. The company predicts HEPLISAV-B revenue of $305-325 million and an adjusted EBITDA of at least $75 million for 2025. Dynavax is focused on developing vaccines to combat infectious diseases.

news.headline: ConnectM forecasts $11.3M revenue in Q1 2025, up 100% YoY, total annual revenue expected at $45.2M. AI-driven solutions drive growth.
news.summary: ConnectM Technology Solutions, Inc. has increased its annual revenue run rate to $45.2 million with the guidance for Q1 2025 at $11.3 million, a 26% increase from Q4 2024 and a 100% surge from Q1 2024. The growth is attributed to expanded customer acquisitions in areas such as Building Electrification, Distributed Energy, and Transportation & Logistics, as well as operational efficiency and high demand for their proprietary AI-driven technology platforms. The company's accelerating momentum is also driven by strategic partnerships and a subscription-based model.

news.headline: Global study by World Ovarian Cancer Coalition finds ovarian cancer costs countries over $70 billion annually, impacting communities worldwide.
news.summary: In Toronto, the World Ovarian Cancer Coalition unveiled a groundbreaking study in partnership with RTI International, shedding light on the substantial socioeconomic impact of ovarian cancer globally. Published in JCO Global Oncology, the report outlines the considerable financial burden faced by communities due to this disease. The study emphasizes the urgency of implementing strategies to enhance the survival rates and well-being of ovarian cancer patients and their caregivers. This research is crucial as it provides insights on the necessary steps to tackle this issue effectively.

news.headline: Akamai reported Q4 2024 revenue of $1.020B, up 3% YoY, with security and compute revenue growing. Full-year revenue was $3.991B, up 5% YoY. GAAP net income in Q4 was $0.91 per diluted share, down 12% YoY, with full-year GAAP net income per diluted share at $3.27, down 7%. Non-GAAP net income in Q4 was $1.66 per diluted share, down 2% YoY, with full-year non-GAAP net income per diluted share at $6.48, up 5% YoY. Operating margin and adjusted EBITDA showed variations, and future guidance was provided for 2025.
news.summary: Akamai Technologies, Inc. reported financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2024. In the fourth quarter, revenue was $1.020 billion, a 3% increase year-over-year. Security and compute revenue represented 69% of total revenue, growing 16% year-over-year. GAAP net income per diluted share was $0.91, down 12% year-over-year. Full-year revenue was $3.991 billion, up 5% year-over-year. Security and compute revenue represented 67% of total revenue, growing 18% year-over-year. GAAP net income per diluted share was $3.27, down 7% year-over-year. The company provided financial guidance for the first quarter and full year 2025, with revenue expected to be between $4.0-4.2 billion and non-GAAP net income per diluted share between $6.00-6.40. Akamai will host a conference call to discuss these results at 4:30 p.m. ET.

news.headline: BJ's Restaurants, Inc. reported FY 2024 results:- Total revenue $1.4B (+1.8%)- $117.1M Adjusted EBITDA (+12.8%)- $8.0M spent repurchasing 234,000 shares- FY 2025 outlook: $205M-$215M in restaurant profit, $127M-$137M EBITDA, $65M-$75M capex, $40M-$50M share repurchases.
news.summary: BJ's Restaurants, Inc. reported its fiscal 2024 fourth quarter and year-end results. In Q4 2024, total revenue increased by 6.4% to $344.3 million, comparable restaurant sales grew by 5.5%, and adjusted EBITDA increased by 21.1% to $33.1 million. The company repurchased around 234,000 common stock shares worth $8.0 million. For fiscal 2024, total revenues rose by 1.8% to $1.4 billion, with adjusted EBITDA up by 12.8% to $117.1 million. Looking ahead to fiscal 2025, management forecasts a 2-3% growth in comparable restaurant sales, expecting restaurant level profit of $205-215 million and adjusted EBITDA of $127-137 million, with share repurchases between $40-50 million. Adjusted diluted net income was $1.47 for the year, a substantial increase from the previous year. The company's Board of Directors approved an increase in share repurchase program by $50 million. As of February 20, 2025, around $83 million remains under the repurchase program. An investor conference call is scheduled for February 20, 2025, to discuss these financial results.Total revenue Q4 2024: $344.3 millionIncrease in comparable restaurant sales Q4 2024: 5.5%Adjustment in EBITDA Q4 2024: 21.1%Total revenue FY 2024: $1.4 billionIncrease in adjusted EBITDA FY 2024: 12.8%2025 Financial Outlook: Comparable restaurant sales growth expected 2-3%, restaurant level profit $205-215 million, adjusted EBITDA $127-137 million, capital expenditures $65-75 million, and share repurchases $40-50 million. Confirmed $83 million remains under the current share repurchase program.

news.headline: GRAIL reported Q4 revenue of $38.3M, sold 137,000 Galleri® Tests in 2024, ended with $767M cash, expanded TRICARE coverage. CEO positive on future growth and commercialization. TRICARE added GRAIL's Galleri test as a covered benefit. Integration with Quest Diagnostics for Galleri test also announced. Moderated cash burn, significant financial results for Q4 and full year 2024 mentioned in detail. MENLO PARK, Calif., Feb. 20, 2025 (PRNewswire) - GRAIL, Inc. (NASDAQ:GRAL) reported total revenue of $125.6 million for full year 2024, Galleri revenue of $108.6 million and net loss of $2.0 billion, aiming for growth in 2025 with focus on efficiency. Conference call/webcast to discuss further details. Full story available on Benzinga.com.
news.summary: GRAIL, Inc. reported selling over 137,000 Galleri® tests in 2024, achieving U.S. Galleri revenue growth of 45% to $108.6 million. The company finished 2024 with a cash position of $767 million. In the fourth quarter, revenue grew 26% to $38.3 million, with net loss of $97.1 million. For the full year, total revenue increased by 35% to $125.6 million, with a net loss of $2.0 billion. TRICARE Health Insurance added Galleri as a covered benefit for patients over 50 with an elevated cancer risk. GRAIL aims for continued commercial growth in 2025. The Galleri test has been well-received, as reported in the Lancet Oncology and available through Quest Diagnostics. A conference call was held on February 20, 2025, to discuss the results and company updates. Non-GAAP measures like Adjusted Gross Profit and Adjusted EBITDA were provided for analysis alongside GAAP figures. This healthcare company focuses on early cancer detection using next-gen sequencing and machine learning.

news.headline: Diginex Limited cross-lists on Frankfurt Stock Exchange & Tradegate Exchange as "I0Q" on Feb. 20, 2025; aims to expand investor base in Europe.
news.summary: On February 20, 2025, Diginex Limited, a Cayman Islands impact technology company focused on ESG matters, revealed that its shares, listed on Nasdaq under "DGNX," are also available on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange (Open Market) and Tradegate Exchange with the symbol "I0Q." This move is anticipated to broaden the company’s investor base globally, improving liquidity and providing European investors with greater access. The cross-listing strengthens Diginex's position in major international financial markets.

news.headline: Viper Energy, Inc. announces leadership transition plan with Travis D. Stice stepping down as CEO. Kaes Van’t Hof to take over as CEO. Austen Gilfillian named President.
news.summary: MIDLAND, TX, 20th February 2025 - Viper Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: VNOM) disclosed its leadership transition plan, following an extensive succession planning process. The change involves Travis D. Stice stepping down as CEO immediately. Kaes Van’t Hof, the current President, will take over as the new CEO. Meanwhile, Austen Gilfillian, the current VP of Viper, will assume the role of President with immediate effect. This transition aims to ensure a smooth shift in leadership and set the Company up for sustained long-term success.

news.headline: Kiniksa Pharmaceuticals (Nasdaq: KNSA) to report Q4 and full year 2024 financial results on Feb 25, 2025 at 8:30 am ET.
news.summary: On February 20, 2025, Kiniksa Pharmaceuticals International, plc (Nasdaq: KNSA) revealed plans to hold a conference call and live webcast on February 25, 2025, at 8:30 a.m. EST. The purpose of the event is to disclose the financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024, as well as discuss recent portfolio activities.

news.headline: BJ’s Restaurants, Inc. announces fiscal Q4 and FY 2024 results, 2025 financial outlook, and $50M share repurchase increase.
news.summary: Reveals financial projections for the year 2025 and authorizes an additional $50 million for share repurchases.

news.headline: Diamondback Energy, Inc. announces CEO transition plan; Travis D. Stice to step down as CEO and become Executive Chairman, Kaes Van’t Hof to succeed; Jere Thompson named CFO.
news.summary: In Midland, Texas, on Feb. 20, 2025, Diamondback Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: FANG) revealed its leadership transition plan to optimize long-term performance. CEO Travis D. Stice, in position since Jan. 2012, will step down at the 2025 Annual Meeting of Stockholders. He will then become Executive Chairman of the Board. Kaes Van’t Hof, the current President, will take over as CEO and join the Board. Jere Thompson, previously the Executive VP of Strategy and Corporate Development, will now serve as the Executive VP and CFO. These changes follow a meticulous succession planning process aimed at maintaining the Company’s success.

news.headline: Travere Therapeutics reports Q4 results, plans to submit sNDA for FILSPARI® in 1Q 2025 for FSGS approval.
news.summary: The company plans to submit a supplemental New Drug Application (sNDA) towards the end of the first quarter of 2025. This application aims to secure traditional approval for FILSPARI® (sparsentan), a medication for Focal Segmental Glomerulosclerosis (FSGS).

news.headline: Xenon Pharmaceuticals (Nasdaq: XENE) to announce Q4 2024 financial results on Feb 27, 2025.
news.summary: In a press release issued on February 20, 2025, Xenon Pharmaceuticals Inc., a biopharmaceutical company with a focus on neuroscience, disclosed its intention to unveil the financial outcomes for the fourth quarter of 2024 after the closure of U.S. financial markets on Thursday, February 27, 2025. The company, listed as Nasdaq: XENE, is dedicated to the exploration, development, and provision of transformative therapeutics for patients facing medical challenges. This announcement was made from Vancouver, British Columbia, and Boston.

news.headline: Asure Software (Nasdaq: ASUR) to announce Q4 and full year 2024 financial results on March 6, 2025 at 4:30 pm ET.
news.summary: In Austin, Texas on February 20, 2025, Asure Software, Inc. (Nasdaq: ASUR), a prominent provider of cloud-based Human Capital Management (HCM) software solutions, disclosed plans for a conference call on March 6, 2025, at 4:30 p.m. Eastern time. The call aims to deliberate on the financial outcomes of the fourth quarter and full year of 2024. The Company will release the financial results through a press release before the scheduled call.

news.headline: WillScot reports Q4 2024 results, provides 2025 outlook. Leader in temporary space solutions, Nasdaq: WSC stock.
news.summary: On February 20, 2025, WillScot Holdings Corporation, a prominent player in temporary space solutions trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol WSC, shared its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024. The update included significant performance achievements and market insights. Alongside this, the Company provided its projections for the entirety of 2025.

news.headline: Sprott to announce 2024 Q4 results on Feb 26, 2025, with webcast to discuss led by CEO, CFO, and Asset Management CEO.
news.summary: Sprott Inc. (NYSE: SII) (TSX: SII) disclosed on February 20, 2025, that it is set to unveil its Q4 2024 financial outcomes at 7:00 a.m. on February 26, 2025. Following this, an earnings webcast has been scheduled for 10:00 a.m. on the same day to delve into the results. Leading the webcast will be Sprott’s CEO, Whitney George, the CFO, Kevin Hibbert, and the CEO of Sprott Asset Management, John Ciampaglia. Interested individuals can access the webcast at the designated time.

news.headline: Petco Health (Nasdaq: WOOF) hosts Q4 2024 Earnings Conference Call on March 26, 2025, at 4:00 p.m. Eastern.
news.summary: In San Diego, a renowned pet health company, Petco Health and Wellness Company, Inc. (Nasdaq: WOOF), is scheduled to announce its fourth quarter earnings for 2024 and full-year fiscal outlook for 2025 on March 26, 2025, at around 4:00 p.m. Eastern Time. This company is known for its comprehensive services dedicated to pet health and wellness.

news.headline: FIU Chaplin School appoints new chair and 3 members to Exec Advisory Board, led by exec from Carnival Cruise Lines.
news.summary: A new chair has been appointed to Florida International University's Chaplin School of Hospitality & Tourism Management Executive Advisory Board in Miami on February 20, 2025. The board welcomed three new members alongside a hospitality executive from Carnival Cruise Lines taking the lead. This move will bring fresh perspectives and expertise to the board, further enhancing its abilities in overseeing the school's operations and strategies.

news.headline: Intermex launches wire transfers via WhatsApp for US Hispanics, providing fast and secure money transfers in trusted app.
news.summary: On February 20, 2025, International Money Express, Inc. unveiled their latest offering by introducing wire transfer services through WhatsApp, catering to the preferences of Latino communities. Recognizing WhatsApp as a widely used messaging application among Latinos, with approximately 95% of U.S. Hispanics utilizing the platform on their smartphones, the move aims to streamline the money remittance process. International Money Express, Inc., traded as NASDAQ: IMXI, is renowned for its omnichannel financial services, with this innovation set to provide a seamless, secure, and efficient means of sending money through a familiar and trusted app.

news.headline: Tile Shop schedules Q4 2024 earnings call.
Inventiva and Hepalys Pharma start clinical development program of lanifibranor in Japan with the dosing of the first participant in Phase 1 trial.
news.summary: Inventiva, a biopharmaceutical company listed on Euronext Paris and Nasdaq under the ticker symbol IVA, along with Hepalys Pharma, Inc., a Japanese company established by Catalys Pacific, have revealed the commencement of a clinical development initiative for lanifibranor in Japan. The program has kicked off with the dosing of the first Japanese participant in a Phase 1 clinical trial aimed at assessing the safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics (PK), and pharmacodynamics (PD) of lanifibranor. This trial focuses on developing oral small molecule therapies to address metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis (MASH) and other diseases with notable unmet medical requirements.

news.headline: Pharming Technologies B.V. now owns 92.70% of Abliva AB; offer not available in certain jurisdictions including US.
news.summary: This press release does not constitute an offer, whether directly or indirectly, in certain jurisdictions including Australia, Canada, Hong Kong, Japan, New Zealand, or South Africa where such offers are prohibited by law. Shareholders residing outside Sweden who wish to accept the Offer mentioned in this release should investigate the applicable regulations and potential tax implications. Specific offer restrictions can be found in the "Important information" section at the conclusion of this press release and in the offer document available on the transaction website at www.raredisease-offer.com. Shareholders in the United States should also review the "Special notice to shareholders in the United States" section at the end of this press release for further details.

news.headline: NV5 Global, Inc. announces Q4 and full year 2024 results with 2025 guidance. Nasdaq: NVEE. Hollywood, Feb. 20, 2025.

(Nasdaq: NVEE), a company specializing in technology, conformity assessment, consulting solutions, and software applications, announced its financial performance for the fourth quarter and full year concluding on December 28, 2024.

news.headline: Radiance Technologies partners with Flynn Advisors LLC in strategic consulting. General (Ret) Charles A. Flynn involved.
news.summary: On February 20, 2025, in Huntsville, Alabama, Radiance Technologies, a prominent company in advanced engineering solutions, made public its collaboration with Flynn Advisors LLC as a strategic consultant. General (Ret) Charles A. Flynn, the founder of Flynn Advisors LLC, who brings extensive experience...

news.headline: Tech companies are increasingly turning to US manufacturing to meet demand with #savings of up to 30%.
Walmart beats Q4 earnings with $180.55B revenue; shares dip on lower guidance. Operating income hits $600B, EPS down to 57-58 cents for Q1 & $2.50-$2.60 for fiscal year 2026. Tariffs impact stock.
news.summary: Walmart Inc announced impressive fourth-quarter results with adjusted earnings per share of 66 cents and revenue of $180.55 billion, beating analyst expectations of 64 cents and $180.01 billion. However, the stock fell due to disappointing forward guidance. Earnings misses are rare for Walmart, with only two since May 2020. The gross margin rate increased by 53 basis points, and operating income rose by 8.3% to about $600 billion. Same-store sales for Walmart U.S. climbed by 4.6%. The first-quarter adjusted EPS is expected to be between 57-58 cents, below the estimate of 64 cents, and for fiscal year 2026, adjusted EPS may range between $2.50 to $2.60, below analysts' consensus of $2.76, partly due to new tariffs. Despite the setbacks, Walmart remains resilient in the face of challenges.

news.headline: Dow falls over 1% with Walmart shares plunging 5% after beating Q4 sales estimates; Energy shares rise by 0.8%.
news.summary: U.S. stocks experienced a downward trend at the end of the trading day, with the Dow Jones index dropping more than 1% on Thursday. The Dow closed at 44,132.13, down by 1.11%, while the NASDAQ fell by 0.54% to 19,947.18, and the S&P 500 also declined by 0.49% to 6,114.11. Energy sector shares saw a rise of 0.8% on Thursday, while financials shares fell by 1.6% during trading. Walmart Inc (NYSE:WMT) witnessed a significant drop of over 5% after releasing its quarterly results. The company's sales for the fourth quarter of FY25 reached $180.55 billion, marking a 4.1% year-on-year growth, surpassing the analyst estimate of $180.01 billion. Adjusted EPS for the same period was reported at 66 cents, exceeding the consensus of 64 cents. Walmart anticipates first-quarter FY26 sales growth of 3% – 4% in CC. For further details, visit Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Consor Holdings LLC acquires Park Engineering, a PM/CM firm in California, expanding infrastructure services.
news.summary: In Miami, Consor Holdings LLC, a top infrastructural engineering and consulting company in North America, disclosed their acquisition of Park Engineering (Park), a renowned program and construction management firm catering to California's public agencies. Park specializes in offering PM/CM services for various projects, including roadway, bridge, water, wastewater line upgrades, and other infrastructure improvements. This strategic move signifies Consor's commitment to expanding its reach and capabilities, by integrating Park's expertise and strong industry connections.

news.headline: Accrete and New Era form strategic partnership: 20,000+ customers gain access to AI solutions.
news.summary: Accrete, a prominent provider of AI solutions, has announced a partnership with New Era Technology, a well-established technology partner, to offer its cutting-edge AI solutions to over 20,000 customers worldwide. The partnership was unveiled in New York on February 20, 2025.

news.headline: Blue Square X launches 110-inch 4K Vivid X Touch display for immersive interaction, transforming spaces into dynamic hubs.
news.summary: Unveiled in Hollywood, Florida on February 20, 2025, the Vivid X Touch VXT110 by Blue Square X promises to revolutionize visual experiences with its 110-inch colossal touch display. This cutting-edge technology aims to transform spaces into dynamic hubs of interaction by offering immersive 4K experiences. Blue Square X, a renowned leader in visual display solutions, is setting a new standard in the industry with this innovation. The Vivid X Touch VXT110 signifies a shift towards larger, more engaging displays, catering to the growing demand for high-quality interactive experiences.

news.headline: A US Judge rules that the Trump Administration can continue mass firings of federal workers.
Cintas sees boost in profits with upgraded rating due to improving technical performance, raising investor optimism.
news.summary: Cintas has seen a boost in its Relative Strength Rating, indicating a positive trend in its technical performance. The company is showing strong financial results thanks to its uniform services, with a significant increase in profits. This upgrade in rating prompts the question of whether this positive momentum will persist in the future.

news.headline: Peabody Engineering introduces 3 new sizes to ProChem® tank line: 300, 400, and 500-gallon tanks.
news.summary: In Corona, California on February 20, 2025, Peabody Engineering, a top producer of industrial plastic tank storage solutions, revealed the expansion of its well-received ProChem® tank line with three additional sizes. The latest tanks will hold 300, 400, and 500 gallons respectively, aimed at enhancing their product range.
